terms.definition: A sequential ensemble method that repeatedly fits shallow trees to the negative gradient of a loss function (pseudo-residuals) and adds each tree to the ensemble with a learning rate.
The targets each new boosting tree is trained to predict—equal to the negative gradient of the loss with respect to the current ensemble output; for MSE they are ordinary residuals y − F(x).
A simple model (typically a shallow decision tree) that is only slightly better than chance alone; boosting combines many weak learners into a strong ensemble.
Scaling each tree's contribution by a learning rate η < 1 so updates are gradual; a standard regularization technique in gradient boosting.
The general algorithm family introduced by Friedman; also the name of classic implementations such as sklearn's GradientBoostingRegressor/Classifier.
